name: parametric_pasture_drought_index_ranch_payout_dispute
premise: Insurance product chief Victor Chen must decide whether to auto-trigger drought payouts to ranchers when satellite pasture-greenness indices cross a threshold while ground soil moisture sensors in three counties still read borderline. Actuary Maya Desai warns paying early drains the parametric pool before peak summer kill-risk. Denying angers ranchers facing feed costs tonight during a heat dome. A reinsurer threatens to exit if discretionary payouts become precedent.

agents.name: Victor Chen
agents.identity: I am the insurance product chief accountable to reinsurance treaties, brand promises of fast parametric relief, and anti-fraud modeling integrity.
agents.goal: I want ranchers solvent without teaching the market that our index can be arbitraged with selective ground-truth disputes.

agents.name: Maya Desai
agents.identity: I am the actuary who priced tail correlation between satellite greenness and cattle mortality and who fears pool exhaustion if we pay early everywhere.
agents.goal: I want payouts held until soil moisture confirms because false green-up from irrigation mist triggered last year's near miss.

choices: I will trigger automatic index payouts immediately and buy facultative reinsurance cover on emergency terms, accepting margin squeeze if facultative markets charge punitive spreads.
I will withhold payouts pending joint field moisture verification within seventy-two hours and advance zero-interest credit lines to ranchers, accepting rage if verification delays hit weekends.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Ranchers blockaded the claims office lobby with hay trailers. Victor Chen must choose between immediate index payouts with facultative reinsurance or withheld payouts with credit lines. What does Victor Chen do?
